key_points.text: Exceptional revenue growth: Q1 FY2027 revenue reached $81,615 million, up 85% from $44,062 million in Q1 FY2026, driven by strong demand for Blackwell computing platform and Data Center accelerated computing.

Strong Compute Networking segment performance: Revenue grew from $39,589 million (Q1 FY2026) to $74,550 million (Q1 FY2027), with Data Center computing driven by Blackwell platform demand and networking growing 142% YoY in fiscal 2026.
High profitability with operating income of $56,276 million in Q1 FY2027 vs $23,694 million in Q1 FY2026, representing 138% growth, demonstrating strong operating leverage.

Full year FY2026 results show Compute Networking revenue of $193,479 million (up 67% YoY) and operating income of $130,141 million (up 57% YoY), with Data Center computing revenue growth of 59% and networking growth of 142%.

citation.quote: Revenue from Data Center computing grew 59% driven by demand for our Blackwell computing platform. Revenue from Data Center networking grew 142%

key_points.text: Increased shareholder returns: Quarterly cash dividend increased from $0.01 per share to $0.25 per share effective May 18, 2026, reflecting confidence in cash generation and capital allocation.

citation.quote: On May 18, 2026, we increased our quarterly cash dividend from $ 0.01 per share to $ 0.25 per share

key_points.text: Geographic diversification with 78% of Q1 FY2027 revenue from United States customers ($63,769 million), improving from 58% in Q1 FY2026, reflecting strong domestic demand.

Significant planned capital investment: $32.4 billion in future data center lease obligations expected between Q2 FY2027 and FY2033, indicating management confidence in sustained demand for AI infrastructure.

citation.quote: Between the second quarter of fiscal year 2027 and fiscal year 2033, we expect to commence leases with future obligations of $ 32.4 billion, primarily for data center leases

key_points.text: Customer concentration in high-growth Data Center/Compute Networking segment: In Q1 FY2027, three direct customers represented 21%, 17%, and 16% of total revenue, primarily from Compute Networking segment, confirming demand from hyperscale cloud providers.

citation.quote: For the first quarter of fiscal year 2027, three direct customers represented 21 %, 17 %, and 16 % of total revenue, all of which was primarily attributable to the Compute Networking segment
